content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of structured, context-aware active learning agent memory can resolve a long-standing flaw in curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (ICM/RND): that intrinsic reward decays rapidly when agents encounter high-density novel but unstructured environments, because there is no mechanism to retain and reuse patterns across prior curious explorations. We hypothesize that tagging episodic curious exploration traces in an active learning agent's structured memory, and letting the agent actively query this memory to prioritize under-explored pattern clusters, will deliver 30-40% higher sample efficiency than vanilla ICM/RND in sparse-reward long-horizon tasks.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
